rationale: Target intake, duplicate replay, provider receipt counts, provider dedupe hits, job lag, job failures, and dead-letter dashboard checks passed.
Dedicated staging PMS runtime proved the legacy scheduler freeze with zero active jobs and nine frozen jobs.
Scheduler-freeze audit rows were inserted into the target database and the post-freeze dashboard reported no missing or unfrozen scheduler jobs.

Provider dashboards remained pointed at legacy URLs; the temporary target runtime stayed observe-only and was stopped after replay, returning the environment to legacy-only provider delivery without a provider dashboard mutation.
The VAY-794 acceptance criteria allow rollback proof through abort-before-switch.
residualRisk: This rehearsal does not prove the future production provider dashboard endpoint switch.
Production cutover still requires a controlled switch window, owner coverage, rollback credentials, and provider dashboard evidence.

ownerAssignments.decision: Accept abort-before-switch rollback proof for VAY-794 rehearsal closure.
Staging target runtime, staging PMS freeze runtime, SSM secret path, ECS runner cleanup, and dashboard report recorded.
Dedicated staging PMS runtime reports scheduler disabled, zero active jobs, nine frozen jobs, and freeze audit rows inserted.
Channex and Stripe provider exports plus controlled replay evidence recorded; Xendit deferred/non-blocking.

signOff.scope: Final VAY-794 C1 rehearsal evidence call only; domain owner role assignments remain accountable for production cutover follow-through.
